excerpt: Islam is built upon seven pillars, the first of which is walāya — and through walāya the rest are known and reached, for the walī is the proof upon them.
source_authority: Fatimid chief judge under al-Muʿizz, 4th/10th century
source_location_hint: Volume 1, Kitāb al-Walāya, opening section

excerpt: The First Intellect (al-ʿAql al-Awwal) is the origin from which the order of being proceeds and the goal to which all return — the first and the last in the world of intelligibles, mirrored in the religious order by the Imam.
source_location_hint: Discourses on the First Intellect as mabdaʾ and maʿād

excerpt: The Universal Intellect (al-ʿaql al-kullī) is the Pen, and the Universal Soul (al-nafs al-kulliyya) is the Preserved Tablet upon which the Pen inscribes the forms that descend into existence.
source_location_hint: Treatment of the Pen and Tablet in the chapters on the universal hypostases

excerpt: The successive imams are each a partial manifestation of the single universal reality of ʿAlī, who is the seal impressed upon every cycle and round of the daʿwa.
source_location_hint: Chapters on the relation of the imams to the universal reality across the cycles

excerpt: By God, were the veil to be lifted, my certainty would not increase.
source_location_hint: Among the short sayings (ḥikam) attributed to Amīr al-Muʾminīn

excerpt: The spiritual heavens, earth, and mountains of the hereafter correspond, rank for rank, to the corporeal heavens and earth of the visible cosmos; the imam is to the believer as the heaven is to the earth that drinks its rain.
source_location_hint: Discourses on the parallel between the physical and spiritual worlds

excerpt: The doctrine of the seven Speaker-Prophets — Adam, Noah, Abraham, Moses, Jesus, Muhammad, and the Qaʾim — is foundational to Ismāʿīlī sacred history, each Nāṭiq inaugurating one of the seven epochs of the world.
source_location_hint: Wellsprings on the cycles of the seven nuṭaqāʾ

excerpt: Qāḍī al-Nuʿmān, in opening his treatment of worship, establishes that every act of religion takes its value from the intention behind it; the inner formation of niyya is the foundation upon which outward obedience rests.
source_location_hint: Volume 1, opening sections on worship and the role of niyya

excerpt: In the Book of Walāya at the head of Daʿāʾim al-Islām, the Imam is identified as the true criterion by which deeds, words, and convictions are appraised, since he alone unites the outward Sharīʿa and the inward truth in a single measure.
source_location_hint: Volume 1, Kitāb al-Walāya (opening book on the Imamate)

excerpt: Nāṣir-i Khusraw expounds the taʾwīl of ṣalāt as the soul's standing in service to the Imam, the outward postures being signs of the inward bond binding the believer to the living guide.
source_authority: Fatimid-era Ismāʿīlī ḥujja of Khurāsān (d. after 465/1073)
source_location_hint: Chapters on the inner meaning of ritual prayer

excerpt: The ḥudūd al-dīn — the ranks of the daʿwa — are ordered from the highest to the lowest, and the service of each rank, however humble, is reckoned by its place in the hierarchy that mirrors the order of the spiritual world.
source_location_hint: discussion of ḥudūd al-dīn and their mirroring of the higher hierarchy

excerpt: The imam is the mufīd absolutely; he imparts and does not receive, while all others are mustafīdūn standing in need of what he confers.
source_location_hint: chapters treating the imam as the source of religious knowledge

excerpt: The qāʾim arises at the end of a cycle, when the duʿāt are few and the people of falsehood overspread the earth; his coming is sudden and lifts the veil of taqiyya.
source_location_hint: treatises on the cycles and the rising of the qāʾim

excerpt: The taʾwīl of tayammum is the bayʿa to the imam: the hand placed upon the pure earth signifies the hand placed in the hand of the imam, by which the believer is purified.
source_location_hint: chapter on the esoteric meaning of purification (ṭahāra) and tayammum

excerpt: In every age the mufīdūn are fewer than the mustafīdūn in the world at large, but within the sanctuary of the daʿwa it is the bearers of instruction who are the strength of the call.
source_location_hint: on the relation of the ḥudūd ʿulwiyya to the ranks below them

excerpt: The Prophet's true Ummah are the imams of his progeny, beginning with the Commander of the Faithful, ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib, and continuing through the infallible imams of his line.
source_location_hint: Volume 1, opening Book of walāya

excerpt: The people of the House have drunk the cup of affliction in this world — slain, exiled, their women widowed and their children orphaned — that the rancour of the hypocrites might be made manifest and they themselves be exalted before God.
source_location_hint: odes lamenting the tribulations of the Ahl al-Bayt

excerpt: Whoever applies to the Originator the attributes of His originated creatures has likened Him to them and so denied His unity; the way of tanzīh requires that He be exalted above both the affirmation and the negation that pertain to created things.
source_location_hint: chapters on tanzīh and the refutation of anthropomorphism

excerpt: The Originator (al-Mubdiʿ) cannot be predicated of by any attribute drawn from the originated order; He is known only through the negation of negation, and tawḥīd properly begins with the confession that He exceeds every cognitive grasp.
source_location_hint: Yanbūʿ on the transcendence of the Originator (tanzīh al-Mubdiʿ)

excerpt: Origination (ibdāʿ) is the bringing-forth of being through the divine command 'Be' (kun) without intermediary, instrument, time, or matter; it is to be distinguished from generation (tawlīd) by which composite beings come to be through the agency of nature.
source_location_hint: Chapter on the distinction between ibdāʿ and tawlīd

excerpt: The doxological summit of knowledge of God is the confession that He cannot be apprehended; the highest cognition is the cognition that cognition fails, and this very recognition of incapacity is itself a form of attainment.
source_author: compiled by al-Sharif al-Radi from sayings of Imam ʿAli
source_location_hint: Opening khuṭba on the impossibility of comprehending the divine Essence

excerpt: The Universal Soul (al-Nafs al-Kulliyya) and the Universal Intellect (al-ʿAql al-Kulliyya) are the two principal Emanators (al-Munbaʿithūn) from the First Intellect, and every partial soul and partial intellect in the cosmos derives its being and power from them.
source_location_hint: Yanbūʿ on the procession of the Universal Soul and Intellect

excerpt: At death the believer's spiritual form is conjoined with its upper limit (ḥadd ʿālī) in the spiritual hierarchy; this conjunction is itself a partial qiyāma, and the soul thereafter inhabits a station of repose and knowledge that is itself a garden of the celestial Paradise.
source_location_hint: Majlis on the believer's soul after death and its joining the higher limit

excerpt: Imam ʿAli said to al-Ḥārith al-Hamdānī: every dying person shall behold me at his death — to the believer I shall appear as glad tidings, and to the disbeliever as a foe.
source_author: compiled by al-Sharif al-Radi from sayings of Imam ʿAli
source_location_hint: Sayings to al-Ḥārith al-Hamdānī on the vision at the moment of death

excerpt: The Prophet's grave and pulpit signify, by taʾwīl, the two stations of concealment and disclosure: ʿAli interiorised and sealed the prophetic sciences within himself (the grave), while the Qāʾim shall openly proclaim them at the end of the cycle (the pulpit); the chain of Imams between them constitutes the garden of Paradise mentioned in the hadith.
source_location_hint: Section on the symbolism of the Prophet's mosque, grave, and pulpit

excerpt: the relation between the inspired text and its living articulator who unfolds its meaning is a recurring axis of Ismaili thought
source_location_hint: thematic — the springs (yanabiʿ) treating revelation and its articulator

excerpt: the elect who follow the Imam of the age are the heirs of every prior remnant whom God preserved against a misled majority
source_location_hint: thematic — recurring discussions of the saved few and majority error
